shared_text:
Odell Ramgal’s Softball
August 1 at 9:12 AM ·

Attention Odell, Saunemin, and St. Paul families,

As I’m sure you’ve all seen, IESA recently overturned their decision to cancel fall sports. So, with that being said, softball is back on (for now)! Softball is open to all girls in 5th-8th grade. I encourage you to share the Odell Ramgal’s Softball page with any families you think may be interested. I will post any important information on there for the time being. We intend to get a practice schedule together as soon as possible, but please be patient with us, as this was just as surprising to us as I’m sure it was all of you. For now, you need to make sure you get those sports physicals taken care of!

We will have open field practices, attendance not mandatory, on Wednesday (8/5), Thursday (8/6), and Friday (8/7) from 4:00-5:30 at the Odell Softball Field. We hope to see many of you out there! ~Coach Wunsch

post_text:
Attention Parent/Guardian(s):

Online registration for the 2020-21 school year has begun. Registration will be completed through Teacherease (www.teacherease.com). If you already have a login for Teacherease, you should have received an email with instructions on how to register your student(s). Be sure to check your spam/other folder for this email. If you have not used Teacherease in the past, please register with your email and we will be able to assist you further.

Should you have any questions, please email Chelsey Bressner at bressnerc@odell.k12.il.us This is a new process for Odell Grade School so we ask for your patience and understanding during this time.

We will also be offering a few days in July to come in and register online if you do not have access to the internet! Those date will be released closer to July.

Thank you
Odell Grade School

post_text:
We have now completed four weeks of school, and the face-to-face education of our students continues to be effective. Since we last provided a public statement about Covid-19, we have seen a consistent rate of absenteeism; however, over the past couple of weeks, our county 14-day rolling average of positive cases has increased. Again, with clear communication with Henry County Public Health, this increase was contained to one business in Henry County. When I look at both pieces of data, it gives me continued confidence in the mitigation practices that we put in place for our district. Below I have shared a diagram that contains important information that we use to make decisions on a daily basis.

Please take a look at the following link for the rest of the update!

http://bit.ly/NL-Week4

Mr. Adrian’s 1st grade students were locked into their lesson about volume, pitch, and vibration. Students were learning how the pitch and volume of something creates a vibration, which creates movement. This little device showed students exactly how that worked as Mr. Matt Adrian manipulated the pitch and volume to make different amounts of rice move.

Across the hallway, Ms. Willet’s 1st grade students were busy having independent reading time and building stamina. Students develop a reading box that contains an assortment of books that are in their independent reading level. It’s great to see students so engaged in their learning!

We are NOT moving to a hybrid plan at this time. This information is in preparation for if we ever need to move to a hybrid plan.

Now that we’ve completed a full week of school we feel it is important to update everyone on our hybrid plan. We want to make sure that everyone understands the expectations of our plan and has a significant amount of time to prepare, in the event that we would need to move to a hybrid model for a period of time during the school year. Childcare is the most important detail for everyone to work out with this model. The purpose behind this model is to help reduce or alleviate the potential growth of Covid-19 symptoms or positive tests within the New London School District.

Please read the full release below!
http://bit.ly/NL-HybridPlan

post_text:
Community Transmission: Substantial Controlled

I know that many districts around the area have been making last minute changes to the start of school, or how they will potentially return to school. The guidance that Iowa Schools were given by the Iowa Department of Public Health and the Iowa Department of Education says, “Community Transmission: Substantial Controlled guidance identifies 15-20% positivity in a county on average over the past 14 days AND 10% absenteeism among students expected for in-person learning with Covid-19 related symptoms, quarantine or positive test results. This 10% absenteeism doesn’t include anyone that may be absent for other reasons.”

Please read the statement below for more info.

https://bit.ly/2EilRki

post_text:
As we count down the remaining days of break, we look to prepare for a new school year. As we all know, this school year will be challenging at times, but we are all optimistic that it will be rewarding, to all, in the end. We feel our Return to Learn Plan is well-designed, focused on student and staff safety, and provides us with the flexibility to work through the many different challenges that will occur throughout the year. We all need to understand that New London, along with every school throughout the state and country, will have cases of students and staff that test positive with the Coronavirus. We all need to be realistic, but we also need to trust the process and procedures that we have developed with the Henry County Public Health Department to handle each case. I don’t mention this to cause anxiety, but I want everyone to be prepared for what will be ahead of us. Listed below are several items that are important for our return.

https://bit.ly/NLCSDBackToSchool

Attention Parents: Henry County Public Health is providing free dental and vision screenings on August 6th from 1:30PM-6:00PM and August 20th from 3:00PM-6:00PM for students entering into Kindergarten and 9th grade.

If your child is entering Kindergarten or 9th grade, proof of a dental
screening is required for students. A screening for kindergarten is valid from age three years to four months after enrollment date. A screening for ninth grade is valid from one year prior to enrollment to four months after enrollment date.,

Children entering into Kindergarten are also required to provide proof of a vision screen. A screening will be valid if it was done up to 1 year prior to enrollment and no later than 6 months after enrollment.

If you are unsure if your child needs a vision or dental screen, please contact me at lindy.williams@nlcsd.org.

Tonight's Regional Semi-Final game location has been moved to North Mahaska at 7 PM.

There were several factors that went into the location of this game. Here is a note from the Iowa Girls High School Athletic Union detailing how the decision was made.

"North Mahaska will host New London in the 1A Region 6 Regional Semi on July 17 at 7 p.m. There was no head to head, both teams were 1-1 against common opponents so it came down to overall winning percentage. North Mahaska 10-7 (.588) vs. New London 8-6 (.571)."

Let's get out tonight and support our team at North Mahaska.

Fans should bring their own chairs or be prepared to stand as some of the seating areas are blocked off to assist with Social Distancing.

From West Burlington Activities Director - Jeff Housel regarding tonight's (7/2) Baseball Game:

In addition to the state's COVID-19 guidelines we will have separate entries for West Burlington and New London. West Burlington spectators will sit in the along the 3rd base and left field side and use the bathrooms attached to the pressbox. New London spectators will sit along the 1st base and right field side and use the bathrooms in the admin building beyond the right field fence.

Only designated players from the defensive team will retrieve foul balls and return to the defensive dugout. West Burlington varsity players will stretch and throw on the practice football field at 6:30 PM and New London players will be allowed to stretch and throw at 6:45 PM.

After the JV game we will take infield followed by New London then we will do a brief senior night presentation, starting lineups and national anthem.

post_text:
June 2, 2020
Attention New London Junior and Senior Students and Parents/Guardians:

At this time, we regret to inform you that the 2020 New London Junior-Senior Prom has been canceled. During this entire process we have explored many different avenues that would allow us to host prom for our students in an environment that was fun, memorable, and safe. As we continued to explore various options and asked for guidance it became apparent that it was almost impossible to follow social distancing guidelines in all prom activities.

This was a difficult decision and one that we did not come to lightly. Our hope was that by waiting, seeking out guidance, and finding alternatives that we could in the end have prom for our students. In the end, the safety of our students and staff is our number one priority.

If you have any questions, please contact Superintendent Chad Wahls at 319-367-0512 or chad.wahls@nlcsd.org.

Sincerely,
Chad Wahls, Scott Kracht, and Todd Palmatier

New London Community School District
April 21 at 5:09 PM ·

With the recent news of school closure for the remainder of the year we are scheduling a time for all students to gather their personal belongings that they may have left prior to March 16th. This Thursday, April 23rd the Elementary and MS/HS buildings will be open for students and parents to collect personal belongings. Our doors will be open from 11:30 - 6:00 and we will monitor the number of people that will be allowed to enter each building at a time. We ask our track athletes to also return their uniforms to the office during this time as well. If you are unable to collect your belongings they will be taken to the Central Office Building until the end of the month. At that time any belongings that are not collected will be donated to GoodWill or thrown away. Middle School and High School students this means your activity lockers as well must be cleaned out or locks will be cut off and items will be removed. We will schedule a Senior checkout date towards the end of May, along with a computer turn in day for all remaining K-11 students.

We have a family with children in our district who lost their home to a fire last night and they lost everything. They are in need of clothes and other essentials. Male: Mens shirt size XXL and pant size 40
Female: Women's shirt size 2XXL/size 18/20, shoe size 11, and pant size 18/20
Girl teenager: Shirt size women's Large, shoe size 9, pant size 14
Boy youth: Shirt size youth Large/ size 18, shoe size youth 8, pant size 18 youth
Infant girl: 24 months

A Night With the Stars
Join us for a night of food and entertainment!

Tickets are now on sale and may be purchased online at PaySchools (http://bit.ly/2I10673) or in the school office. If paying online make sure to pay attention to the date, as the show is Friday AND Saturday.
Hurry--reserved seating is limited!
--Front row V.I.P reserved seating with dinner is $30
--Non-reserved seating with dinner is $20.
--Paparazzi seating (bleachers) without a meal is $10 for adults and $5 for children. No passes.

Dinner includes a choice of spaghetti and meatballs with a red sauce OR fettuccine alfredo, salad, and a breadstick. Drink choices are water, tea, lemonade.

post_text: Attention kids 9-12: Mrs. Alspach and Mr. Kracht will be taking a bus to the first annual Iowa Works Southeast Iowa STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) Expo in Middletown during the morning of March 30th. There will be 10 different presenters from employers in Southeast Iowa. The goal with this event is to give students an idea of what types of STEM careers are available right here in Southeast Iowa. Students will find out what type of day to day activities the presenters have to do in their jobs with these companies, along with the education required to attain this career. This expo will give students the chance to network with local employers for possible internships down the road. If you are interested in attending, please let Mrs. Alspach know right away! #NLTigerPride

Updated: HAT DAY

Bring a Donation, Wear a Hat

Friday, February 14, 2020

New London Community Schools would like to have a Hat Day on Thursday. We’re asking students and staff to consider bringing a donation to help one of our families whose son is struggling with some significant medical issues. He was born with a condition that requires a lot of specialized care. He is currently in the NICU at the University of Iowa, but will soon be staying in Des Moines for an extended period. The following link (https://gf.me/u/xiwpus) has more details. Any donation will be very much appreciated. Go Tigers! #nltigerpride

post_text:
ON TO STATE! Congratulations to the students on their performances at the District Large Group Speech Contest this past Saturday in Lone Tree. Their hard work and dedication earned them Division I ratings, advancing them to State Contest on Feb. 8th at Cedar Rapids Washington High School. The One-Act Play Asylum, the Short Film Through My Eyes, and the Group Improvisation "Carter-Little" will compete with the hopes of receiving not only another Division I rating, but also a nomination to the Large Group Speech All-State Festival.

One-Act Play Cast : Maclane White, Griffin Berndt, La'Nya Franklin, Joseph Hedgecock, Elijah Curry, Samuel Denney, Reed Kempker, Alec Smith, Victoria Zaragoza, Addison Brown, Rachel Carter, & Annie Sines(Crew/Spotlight)

Short Film Cast: Addison Brown (Co-writer/Student Director), Victoria Zaragoza (Co-writer/Student Director), Griffin Berndt, Ryan McLerran, & Joseph Hedgecock

Group Improvisation Cast: Rachel Carter & Gwendolynn Little

Friends Supporting Friends
September 8 at 11:51 AM ·

During the recovery efforts in our area, there are not only families displaced, but the contractors and workers that are diligently working to help our community. Friends Supporting Friends is currently housing approximately 40 contractors.

We are asking our community for assistance. We are in need of cots or air mattresses, bedding, cleaning supplies, laundry services as well as food, snacks and drinks.

We are a non profit organization so any monetary donations would go to providing meals and supplies.

If you are interested in donating food for a meal we do have volunteers that are willing to cook.

For more information feel free to call; Shirley Fruge 337-275-6340 or Rhonda Fontenot 337-224-6563.

Any other members that would like to be on the contact list, just put your information in the comments.

FREE ASSISTANCE WITH FIBER REINFORCED SHEETING TO COVER DAMAGED ROOFS IN THE PARISH

Hello, this is an important message from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. Operation Blue Roof is a priority mission managed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers for FEMA. Operation Blue Roof aims to provide homeowners in disaster areas with fiber reinforced sheeting to cover their damaged roofs until permanent repairs are made. If you need a Blue Roof, use your computer or smart phone and contact the website www.usace.army.mil/blueroof for online signup.

shared_text:
McNeese State University
September 3 at 5:21 PM ·

Campus Update from Dr. Daryl Burckel, McNeese President (9.3.2020)

We are working diligently to put together information and guidance for students, faculty and staff about a variety of topics including changes to the academic calendar, financial aid and scholarships, timesheets and payroll, the status of buildings, a plan for students and employees to enter buildings and retrieve belongings and refunds.

Our Hurricane Laura Response Team meets daily, often multiple times in smaller groups. Many employees in other areas, such as Human Resources, Enrollment Management, Business Affairs and Student Services, have been working remotely to pull together information that will hopefully answer many of your questions.

We will be working through the weekend to compile this information and plan to have a more comprehensive guidance and direction ready to disseminate to students, faculty and staff on Monday.

Stay safe, stay healthy and stay Cowboy Strong!

shared_text:
State Representative Troy Romero
September 3 at 6:13 PM ·

I got a Birdseye view of the District this afternoon thanks to the Governor and his staff. The Governor visited Jeff Davis Parish and Beauregard Parish today. I wanted to make sure he got to see the devastation we are all dealing with. I also wanted him to be aware of the massive amount of debris that has ended up in our drainage. It takes widespread cooperation from all agencies to get through a disaster like this one. The good news is, if we all pull in the same direction we will get through this. Thanks for your patience and cooperation during this trying time. And a big thanks to the Governor and his staff.

We will post on Facebook when they become available.

Please be patient as these items and food are donated and we are doing our best to accommodate you.

Cathy Kendall shared a post to the group: Jeff Davis Parish Emergency Communication.
August 24 at 6:28 PM ·

Jeff Davis Electric CO-OP
August 24 at 10:47 AM ·

JDEC is monitoring both Marco and Hurricane Laura at this time. It looks like we will feel the impacts from both of these in our service area. Please be prepared for these storms. Power outages can be expected, so please take every precaution necessary, especially those who rely on life support. JDEC has already started emergency preparations and is on standby for what is to come.
Once storms hit, it could cause electric lines to be torn down. Please do not attempt to touch or move downed lines, this can be deadly. High winds and storm conditions can cause widespread outages in which a timeframe for restoration can be difficult to determine. JDEC employees will do our best to answer all phone lines and any questions you may have. Remember, our Facebook messenger is not monitored. Please call our office.
Call 337-824-4330 or 1-800-256-5332 to report outages, downed lines, or any other hazardous situations. JDEC will be working around the clock to restore and repair all damages that may occur from these storms. Everyone please stay safe.

Hey it’s that time again. We here at Hobo’s, with Jeff LaBouve (Jeff’s AC and Heating) and Paul Dupuis will be cooking again tomorrow night. But this time Michael Cassidy, Jennings Tractor and Equipment(Corey Cormier and Ellen Daigle Cormier), and Guillotte Brothers (Frank and Steve Guillotte) will be providing the meal. It’s awesome that these 3 sponsors have stepped up to the plate to provide a plate of food for our health care members! They are providing meals for the Jennings American Legion Hospital, Jennings Police Department, Jeff Davis Parish Sheriff’s Office, JenningsFire Department and anyone that works in a related field. (EMT’s, First Responders, etc.)

We will be delivering meals to each place above starting at 5pm but we want to provide a meal to you if you are not working at said place. So please swing by Hobo’s and pick a meal up.

We are looking forward to serving you again!

Please share this post and tag people that work in the field above so we can provide a meal to them in this pandemic.

Small businesses are the foundation of our communities and our economy. Employing nearly half the American workforce, companies like yours keep our neighborhoods running and make them feel like home.

We know you’re facing multiple challenges right now, and every dollar counts.

An initiative of the US Chamber of Commerce Foundation, funded by corporate and philanthropic partners, the Save Small Business Fund of the US Chamber of Commerce is a collective effort to provide $5,000 grants to as many small employers as we can. We hope these supplemental funds will help you get through the next days and weeks.TO
-
To qualify you must
- Employ between 3 and 20 people
- Be located in an economically vulnerable community
- Have been harmed financially by the COVID-19 Pandemic

post_text: Carey Sovereign, a senior at STEM Charter Academy, was invited to attend the Summer Idaho Science & Aerospace Scholars Academy sponsored by Boise State University and NASA. The one week July 2019 session took place at Boise State and at NASA's Ames Research Center in Mountain View, CA. Carey had the opportunity to work with students from around the state in a simulated space mission while learning more about post-secondary STEM programs and internships offered by BSU and NASA.

April 12, 2018 at 6:28 PM ·

The DaVinci CubeSat has been delivered to Rocket Lab in Huntington Beach, CA, with a successful integration completed this afternoon. Thank you to the engineers at Clyde Space for their hard work and expertise during the construction and integration of this CubeSat. Thank you to NASA - National Aeronautics and Space Administration for awarding this student-led team at North Idaho STEM Charter Academy the opportunity to participate in the CubeSat Launch Initiative Program, and for the enduring support throughout this CubeSat's construction. Photos and more details of today's integration will be released next week, and updates will be shared as the launch date nears.

post_text: Air quality update: Moving forward we will treat this like we do extreme cold or rainy days. We will make that call on a daily basis, and always err on the side of student safety. If the air quality is still unhealthy, we will still have the students go to the cafeteria or gym first thing in the morning.
text: Air quality update: Moving forward we will treat this like we do extreme cold or rainy days. We will make that call on a daily basis, and always err on the side of student safety. If the air quality is still unhealthy, we will still have the students go to the cafeteria or gym first thing in the morning.

post_text:
The smoke from surrounding wildfires has caused our air quality to dip into the "Very Unhealthy" category. We will still have school, but recesses and PE will all be indoors. Regular recesses will be in classrooms. Lunch recess will be in the gym. I have spoken with Mr. Hatrock, and he will have PE in the gym so our students will still be getting some activity.

At this time, it looks like we will be doing this through at least Wednesday. Obviously, we will continue to monitor the situation and adjust as necessary.

I know this is not the best way to start the school year, but keeping students safe doesn't come with a schedule. Thank you for your flexibility and positive attitude. This too shall pass.
Thank you and have a great day!!!

We are the only high school in the world to have been selected by NASA to send our satellite to space along with institutions like MIT and Stanford. The value of this award is around $5 million, and we need to raise the last $250,000 to cover materials and expenses. Dinner & Auction tickets are $50, but STEM Charter families have a special price of only $30 each. post_text: Congratulations to Mr. Moon for receiving a $1,500 Grant from Kootenai Electric Coop. With this grant, Mr. Moon has purchased a 3D printer for students and teachers to prototype projects. Students will learn about various 3D modeling computer software applications that they can use to design and print their creations. Students will also be printing prosthetic hands through the e-Nable program. There is a whole world of possibilities just waiting to be found.

post_text:
OUT OF THIS WORLD NEWS FOR OUR SCHOOL!!!!
Our high school CubeSAT satellite team has been selected as one of only 20 institutions in the WORLD. Indeed, we are the ONLY HIGH SCHOOL IN THE NATION that has been selected to send our Cube Satellite to space. We already have a launch date of June 2017. This is a tremendous opportunity for our students. As one of our technical advisors said: other schools may have sports teams, but they don’t have a satellite!!!!
http://www.nasa.gov/feature/nasa-announces-seventh-round-of-candidates-for-cubesat-space-missions

Moving Day Update

We’ve had several people ask if their children are helping move as well. As much as we appreciate your help, it is probably best if younger students (K-3) are not helping move things. So we are asking, if at all possible, that you make other arrangements for them during this time as we will not be able to provide supervision for them. We want them to be safe and also, there is only so much room on the sidewalks where heavy items are being transported. Older students (4-8) may help if they would like and have your permission.

Thanks again everybody…it’s getting pretty exciting around here!!!

post_text:
STEM Charter Academy goes K-12

In answer to your repeated requests and overwhelming support of this school, we have been working for months with the State Department of Education and the Idaho Public Charter Commission on expanding STEM Charter to high school. We were approved this morning by the Idaho Public Charter Commission to move forward.

We will begin with 9th grade next year and add a grade level each year through 12th grade. We will hold a meeting in late January or early February to provide specific details of our high school program. This is a very exciting day for the students of North Idaho!!!! Again, thank you for your support and your wonderful children!

Scott and Colleen Thomson
STEM Charter Academy
A Free K-12 Public School of Choice

post_text:
Attn: Parents/Guardians:

Late this afternoon we were notified by the Washington County Health Department that we have an individual at John Evans Middle School identified as being a positive case of SARS-CoV-2/Coronavirus (COVID-19).

Due to our contact tracing we were able to immediately identify and isolate all individuals who are considered close contacts. We have contacted all close contacts and their parents/guardians. The close contacts will also be contacted by the Washington County Health Department.

Close contacts are defined as those being closer than 6’ for 15 minutes or longer. People that are NOT close contacts are at low risk of developing COVID-19.

Due to how late in the day it was we were not able to send letters home today but buildings will send out a letter tomorrow if your child was in a classroom, bus or cohort group with this individual.

post_text:
The Washington County Health Department is announcing a Public Health Alert due to a possible COVID- 19 community exposure.
Prior to being diagnosed, a COVID-19 positive guest of a student attended the following event:
Potosi High School Graduation in Potosi, MO on June 27th 2020.
The Potosi R-3 School District has been notified and are working closely with the Health Department to notify the public.
Close contacts of the case have been notified; however, any person attending the event should diligently monitor for symptoms of COVID-19 through July 11th. Close contact is defined as being within 6 feet of a confirmed case for at least 15 minutes.
Persons who are NOT close contacts are at a low risk for developing COVID-19. If you attended the event but are NOT a close contact, you do NOT need to quarantine unless you develop symptoms. If you become ill, please contact your Healthcare provider.
The State of Missouri has a hotline number that you can contact at 877-435-8411 and speak with a COVID-19 expert.

On behalf of the Board of Education, I am very pleased to announce that Mr. Alex McCaul will be the new Superintendent for the Potosi R-3 School District. Mr. McCaul is the current Superintendent of the Kingston K-14 School District, and an alumnus of the Potosi R-3 School District. Mr. McCaul has a background in both education and business and has been a leader, friend, and positive example to many in our community. The Board is excited that he has accepted the offer to lead the R-3 District as it endeavors to offer a quality education for the students in our wonderful District.

In the near future, Mr. McCaul will be formally introduced to the District's staff members and begin his journey in fulfilling his dream of becoming a permanent member of the R-3 Team that "Loves Purple and Lives Gold".

RD

R-3 Board of Education Vacancy Filled

After formal interviews of interested candidates, the Board of Education selected Rebekah Jones to fill the vacancy on the Board vacated by Kathy Silvey. Mrs. Jones is a mother of two district children, and is currently employed as Chief Operating Officer at Great Mines Health Center. In this position she oversees 60 employees and manages a budget of 8.5 million dollars in revenue.

The Board welcomes Mrs. Jones to her new position and looks forward to working with her in her new role as a member of the R-3 Board of Education.

The Board of Education and Dr. McCue have mutually agreed to part ways, his last day with the District is August 9, 2019. The Board would like to thank Dr. McCue for his service to the District and wishes him well in his future professional pursuits. The Board welcomes back Mr. Randy Davis who will serve as Interim Superintendent. The Board is committed to a succession plan and superintendent search in a manner which ensures a smooth transition for our staff, students, and families. We see this as an opportunity to regroup, re-energize and refocus on our shared mission to serve students.

post_text:
Potosi R-3 Bond Update

Our final rates feature an interest rate of 3.00% for maturities March 1, 2021, through March 1, 2024, with reoffered yields ranging from 1.70% to 1.90%. The bonds maturing March 1, 2025, through March 1, 2039, carry a 5.00% interest rate with reoffered yields from the March 1, 2024 call date ranging from 2.00% to 2.35%. This plan produces a reoffering premium of $480,925.50, which produces $60,495 greater revenue than what was projected at the May 30, 2019 board meeting. The average interest rate of 4.898% is unchanged; however, the highest reoffered yield of 2.35% is well below the limit of 2.60%.

Some bond issue roofing projects are already underway at the Elementary School. This is one of four buildings roofing projects that we plan to complete this summer. The bond projects will be completed in phases due to specific need completion times and logistics of the school year.
We are excited to get these projects underway.

A BIG “Thank You” to our community for your support of our children’s education through the bond issue.

post_text:
Missouri State Teachers Foundation February Classroom Grant winners

Congratulations to Melinda Saunders from Potosi Elementary for winning February’s $100 Classroom Grant from the Missouri State Teachers Foundation!
“Our school district has implemented a new Tutoring Program called TAP which will allow every student in our district to have the opportunity to stay for tutoring after school with transportation provided,” Saunders wrote in her application. “I would use the $100.00 to purchase STEM activities for the students to use during tutoring. STEM activities will allow the students the opportunity to work together as a team to explore, research, experiment, and use communication skills together to work to solve the task given. STEM allows the ability for all students, at-risk and high flyers, to use what they already know along with the new knowledge learned to successfully complete each task as they follow steps to complete the project.”

Congratulations again, Melinda!

post_text:
Creating a LOVE of READING and BOOKS

Here at Potosi, we want to create and foster a HUGE LOVE of READING and BOOKS within our students. We are giving our elementary and intermediate students a home library to decorate and a book to bring home. Our goal is to fill their libraries with fun books that will inspire them to love to read. We hope that this will also inspire family members, friends and community members to help with our goal of creating a HUGE LOVE of READING and BOOKS in our students. Giving students their own books to collect and read builds enthusiasm and helps cultivate a lifelong love of reading. Reading is the fundamental skill that will allow all children to reach their potential and follow their DREAMS.

Potosi R-3 would like to address our community and the tragedy that occurred at Kingston K-14 this morning. We want to communicate our sincerest and deepest sympathies to all family, friends and staff affected by this event in our neighboring district.

Potosi R-3 has made available services if needed, which may aid in Kingston’s process through this tragic event. We have also initiated our Crisis Intervention Teams within our buildings if needed to address any emotional needs of our students.

If you have any questions or are in need of assistance please do not hesitate to contact any building office.

Respectfully,

Shawn McCue Ed. D.
Superintendent of Schools

post_text:
Potosi High School is pleased to partner with our Washington County first-responders to practice emergency response procedures and to evaluate and improve upon the effectiveness of those procedures. A county-wide disaster drill will be practiced at different locations on Monday, October 16. The leaders in Washington County have adopted a joint Mass Casualty Response Plan and triage system of care that will be utilized during the drill.

PHS students will participate in a school-wide tornado drill at 9:00 a.m. and then continue with their normal school routine and schedule. The high school gymnasium will be utilized by first responders as a triage location. Selected theater students, with parent permission, will participate in the drill as patients.
Potosi R-3 is pleased to partner with the community and provide a facility to practice this important drill.

Meg Schebler‎Camanche Activity Department
August 31 at 1:58 PM ·

Hey all Camanche fans!

We’re excited to kick off our home activities tonight with the JV football game, as we host the Mustangs of Mt. Vernon.

As of this morning, we are REQUIRING FACE MASKS/FACE COVERINGS at all home activities at the Middle School and High School. This includes outdoor activities as well. Pleas help us by following these protocols so our students can continue to participate in events and so we won’t have to limit spectators!

We also ask that you sit with your family, including children younger than 9th grade, and adhere to social distancing guidelines. Please don’t congregate by restrooms, concessions stands, or ticket booths.

For Football games, you may bring lawn chairs and spread out down the fence line. For Volleyball games we will have both sides of the bleachers available in order to social distance better.

Kick off is 6pm tonight, see you there!

The Camanche School Board had their regular monthly meeting last night. At that meeting the Board reached the decision to require all people present on school property to wear face coverings. There is an exception to this requirement for people with a medical exemption, or if a school administrator determines that an extenuating circumstance exists. These types of situations will be handled on a case by case basis.

The goal of the Camanche School District is to keep all people who are present in our facilities as safe and healthy as possible, while still providing our students with a quality education. Please feel free to contact your building administrator or me with any questions or concerns.

post_text: I am pleased to announce that Registration is now open in the Camanche School District for the 2020-2021 school year. Families that are currently in our student accounting system should also receive an email providing a link to the registration page. If you wish to directly access the registration page, go to our district’s website at www.camanche.k12.ia.us. For those who wish to apply for free and reduced lunch, the application can be completed on our registration site. Finally, by the end of this week (7/10/2020) we plan to add a Return to Learn (RTL) information page on our website. I would encourage parents in our district to look for and complete the Parent Survey that will be located in that link. Your input about our RTL Plan is important and will assist us in meeting our goal of providing a safe and quality learning environment for your child. As always, feel free to contact any of our school offices with any questions or concerns.

Camanche Activity Department
April 27 at 9:15 PM ·

Senior Spotlight – Markee Low

I would have been involved in track this spring

What is something from your time at CHS that you will always remember?
I will always remember practices with my best friends and amazing coaches. I will also remember the exciting feeling of meets and watching my times drop throughout the season.

What advice would you give to underclassmen?
The advice I would give to underclassmen is to practice and perform at every meet like it is your last because you don’t know when it could be. Also to cherish the times that you have with your friends and coaches before it is too late.

What does it mean to be a Camanche Indian?
To me being a Camanche Indian is to be surrounded by people that are constantly supporting you. I cannot thank my friends and parents enough for pushing me to be the best I can be. Also I would like to thank Coach Ford for always being there for me and helping me to achieve all of my goals.

April 22 at 10:10 AM ·

Senior Spotlight - Dasana Moore

I would have been involved in track and field this year!

What are your plans after high school?
My plans after high school are going to CCC in the fall to pursue a nursing degree.

What is something from your time at CHS that you will always remember?
I will always remember the time when Senora admitted she liked having me as a student hahaha love you Sratty.

What advice would you give to underclassmen?
The advice I would give is “Be the change everyone is always talking about. You don’t have to have a loud voice to be a leader, start right where you are and make change happen :).”

What does it mean to be a Camanche Indian?
To me being a camanche Indian is having pride from where I come from. It’s having grit and strength on the nights your pulling an all nighter to get homework done. Being an Indian is forming not only life long friends, but having a family you can always lean on🤍.

Governor Reynolds announced today that schools are to remain closed for the rest of this school year. She also stated that "Continuous Learning" for students is to continue. In Camanche we will continue to engage and educate our students by using on-line learning. I also want you to know that we are committed to getting the information you need to support your child’s learning to you as soon as we possibly can. I encourage you to contact your child’s principal with questions or concerns that you may have.
Finally, I want to express my sincere thanks to everyone who is doing their part in helping us address the challenges this pandemic has created. Your efforts and support are truly appreciated. I hope that all of you are able to stay healthy and well. Thank you.

I do sincerely appreciate everyone’s understanding as we work through this Pandemic and hope that all of you remain healthy and well.

Camanche CSD worked with the state to qualify for a summer food program during this time of crisis. We were unsuccessful in this work due do not meeting some of the requirements. So, just like we do each summer, please see information below regarding the food program in Clinton being implemented next week.

The Clinton Community School District is participating in an emergency extension of the Summer Food Service Program, offering meals during this time of mandatory school closing. Meals will be provided to all children without charge and are the same for all children regardless of race, color, national origin, sex, age or disability, and there will be no discrimination in the course of the meal service. Meals will be provided, at a first come, first serve basis, at the sites and times as follows:

The following sites will be serving sack lunches from 11:30 am to 1:00 pm, Monday through Friday, starting March 23, 2020 through April 9, 2020:

Clinton Middle School 1350 14th Street NW

Clinton High School 817 8th Avenue South

Be Safe,

Camanche Admin Team

post_text:
Updates regarding COVID-19 Pandemic:

1) Camanche Schools will be closed in accordance with the governor’s recommendation that schools close for four weeks. At this time, we plan to resume classes no earlier than Monday, April 13th.
2) Due to the deep cleaning occurring in our school buildings, we are limiting access to our buildings and request that you contact the building principal before entering a school. We also ask that you pick up any items you need no later than Thursday, March 19th by noon.
3) The State Athletic and Activity Associations have canceled all practices and events at this time. Information regarding dates when these activities will be allowed to resume will be announced by these groups in the future.

We do know that there are more unanswered questions than actual answers available at this time. This has been caused by the rapidly changing events surrounding the COVID-19 Pandemic. We will continue to provide more information to you as it becomes available.

post_text: Great opportunity for Camanche Middle School!
shared_text:
Arconic Davenport Works
February 21 at 3:07 PM ·

The Arconic factory at Junior Achievement of the Heartland BizTown is officially open thanks to a ribbon cutting ceremony this morning by Biztown’s “mayor” and BizTown's Arconic “CEO.” While DPW Manufacturing Manager Cindy Barnett was there to say a few words beforehand, the kids were ready to get right to work afterward.

Davenport Works and Arconic Foundation are proud to be longstanding partners with JA and see our name on the JA BizTown Factory. As Cindy said before the ribbon cutting, “These kids are our future employees. We’re excited to work alongside them and make this opportunity available to help them learn and grow.”

Great opportunity for Camanche Middle School!

Davenport Works and Arconic Foundation are proud to be longstanding partners with JA and see our name on the JA BizTown Factory. As Cindy said before the ribbon cutting, “These kids are our future employees. We’re excited to work alongside them and make this opportunity available to help them learn and grow.”

post_text:
Families-

Exciting news for our 8th grade students! We have scheduled a field trip on Oct. 1st to Black Hawk College and Augustana College.

While at Black Hawk college we will participate in the Manufacturing Day Event. At this event, multiple employers and educational institutions will demonstrate manufacturing technologies and processes to increase awareness of the possible career opportunities within the manufacturing industry. Students will have the opportunity to experience the technologies, hands-on, through interactive stations.

After our morning at Black Hawk we will travel to Augustana College for a campus tour and an admissions presentation!

Together In Education,

Mr. Shaffer

post_text:
Virtual Academy of Allegan County (VAAC)

The Virtual Academy of Allegan County (VAAC) is being designed in a collaborative effort among local, participating school districts to provide a 100% online educational option for students and families. It is important to note that this is vastly different from both a traditional in-person school experience and the spring 2020 emergency remote instruction. The VAAC provides online curriculum, interactive instruction and activities. There will be assessments and students will earn grades and credits as part of staying connected to their local district. If you are interested in potentially enrolling your child in VAAC please respond to this survey.

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1f_v5DOiBzUJfjtF4F8Y0azL_qJgGQg2cAIKIKELyczI/viewform?edit_requested=true

post_text: Artwork created by HES Kindergartener, Kade Byville, was exhibited in the Michigan Visual Arts Celebration Feb. 7-12, 2020. Kade’s Art piece was chosen for further adjudication at the State Level! The Visual Arts Celebration, hosted by the GRAM, featured Artwork by K-8th grade students from 24 public and private schools in Region 9 of Michigan. Students and families were invited to tour the exhibit, and recognize the Artists at a reception on Thursday, Feb. 6. Artwork chosen by the next jury will participate in the State Show.

post_text: Hopkins FFA Chapter hosted donkey basketball on Sat. Feb. 1 at the high school gym in order to raise money to help provide leadership development opportunities for our members. Four teams competed, including students, staff, FFA members, and the Hopkins Area Fire Department. Participants had to ride donkeys while playing basketball. Rules included you could not let your donkey go free, one person had to be on their donkey in order to pass, and you had to be on your donkey in order to shoot the ball. The HAFD were named the champions for the third time in a row! A huge thank you to all of our participants and all those who came out to enjoy the show!

text:
Hopkins FFA Chapter hosted donkey basketball on Sat. Feb. 1 at the high school gym in order to raise money to help provide leadership development opportunities for our members. Four teams competed, including students, staff, FFA members, and the Hopkins Area Fire Department. Participants had to ride donkeys while playing basketball. Rules included you could not let your donkey go free, one person had to be on their donkey in order to pass, and you had to be on your donkey in order to shoot the ball. The HAFD were named the champions for the third time in a row! A huge thank you to all of our participants and all those who came out to enjoy the show!

Hopkins FFA
February 3 at 10:51 PM ·

Donkey Basketball (part 5)...Championship round...Students vs Fire Fighters

Congrats to the HAFD on keeping their winning streak going and being 3X Donkey Basketball Champions🎉🏀🏆🎉

post_text: November was first declared as National Homeless Youth Awareness Month in 2007. Since then, November has been a time to acknowledge those children and families experiencing homelessness. As many as 2.5 million youth per year experience homelessness. Along with losing their home, community, friends, and routines as well as their sense of stability and safety, many homeless youth are also victims of trauma.

post_text:
Third graders at Hopkins Elementary participated in the Bonnie Plants' Third Grade Cabbage Program this summer. Students received a giant-variety cabbage plant from Bonnie Plants, as well as instruction from the Hopkins FFA School Garden team to grow the biggest cabbage they could. The Hopkins FFA provided an additional incentive of an M-89 Cinema gift card and goody pail for the Hopkins student with the largest cabbage. Congratulations to Cole Erickson, whose cabbage weighed in at over thirteen pounds! Cole will also be entered in Bonnie Plants' national scholarship competition for the largest cabbage nationwide.

Third graders continue to learn science lessons in the school garden this fall with the Hopkins FFA School Garden team.

post_text:
ACT – Allegan County Tours for Freshmen
The Allegan Area STEM Partnership, AAESA, and local district volunteers, are coordinating a countywide effort to provide two employer tours to every Allegan AESA district freshman during the month of October. This project is an attempt to provide an intentional progression of experiences for students to explore various career paths, employment opportunities in the Allegan County region, and to help districts meet the Michigan Career Development requirements. In this inaugural year, there are 1,150 freshmen students from 10 schools in Allegan County participating.

post_text: The high school band wrapped up the school year with their Memorial Day performances. We visited both Maplewood and Poplar hill and spent time remembering those who gave their lives for our country and fought for our freedom. We were honored to perform the National Anthem and the piece In the Shining of the Stars. Senior trumpet player Laura Velderman also performed taps for the ceremonies and it was covered by the news! Such a beautiful way to end a great year!

post_text: Hopkins 7th & 8th Grade Band students performed at Michigan's Adventures on May 31st this year. They performed three pieces for the crowds while they worked their way through the main gate. They then got to enjoy the part for about 5 hours after lunch. It was a beautiful day for the park! The combined group of around 70 7th and 8th grade students had a blast!

Hopkins Elementary's schedule for student items/learning material distribution
Location: Hopkins Elementary Bus Loop
Y5/K and 1st Grade: Monday, April 20th 9:00am-12:00pm
2nd and 3rd Grade: Monday, April 20th 5:00-7:00pm
4th and 5th Grade: Tuesday, April 21st 10:00am-12:00pm
(If a family has siblings in the building, they can pick up all of their children's materials at one time.)

Parents, please do not leave your vehicles. If you are ill or have been exposed to someone who is ill in the last 48 hours, please contact your building principal and make alternate arrangements for pickup. You will be greeted by a staff member who will "take your order" and radio the student information to staff members inside the building. We will gather all belongings and needed academic materials and deliver it to you curbside. This will eliminate the need for the community to enter the building. Staff members will be following social distancing guidelines including wearing masks and gloves.
Please follow the recommended times to help us complete this process in a timely manner.

post_text:
On Thursday, January 23rd Don Cochrane from Camp Kaskitowa in Allegan will be joining the 3rd grade classrooms for a Michigan History presentation.

Michigan 1763 Living History Presentations:

Michigan Living History presentation; like a field trip in your own classroom. Meet a Coureur de Bois (French-Canadian
Independent fur trader) who will interact with your students and help them understand what life was like in Michigan 250 years ago. Understand the impact Native American cultures (Potawatomi, Ojibwa, Ottawa, and Huron) and European Colonial cultures (French, British) had on each other in 1763 during Michigan’s colonial period. Also, learn about the tools and skills needed to survive in the North Woods over 250 years ago. Presenter will be in historically correct dress and have tools of the trade for living in the wilderness including tomahawks, knives, and musket with black powder.

shared_text:
HME Ahrens-Fox
December 19, 2019 at 4:53 PM ·

Members of the HME Ahrens-Fox family were blessed this week with the great privilege of escorting students and faculty of Hopkins Public Schools in a delivery of hope for the holidays to the patients of the Helen DeVos Children's Hospital. This annual charity drive is organized by Jason and Allison Babiak as a means of paying forward and honoring a similar kismet act of kindness granted to their son in 2014. Dawson Babiak, a cancer survivor and former patient of HDCH, was gifted a package of window markers while receiving treatment. This simple display of compassion provided him with a jumpstart towards recovery. Five years later, through the generosity of the families and students of the Hopkins Public School District, a single package of markers has grown to an entire bus loaded with toys.

We are grateful for the opportunity to join the Babiak family and the students from HPS in this heartwarming display of consideration towards those who can truly benefit from it this holiday season.

SCHOOL UPDATES:

The health and safety of our students, our staff and their families is our TOP priority. We want students and staff to feel comfortable and safe returning to campus. Our plan incorporates recommendations from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), Arizona and Maricopa County health departments, Arizona Department of Education, and state and local guidelines

Maintaining Healthy Environments @ East Valley High School

-Social Distancing precautions in each classroom & throughout the building

-Sanitizer stations in each classroom & throughout the building

-Sneeze guards throughout the lab classrooms and front desk

-Fresh paint throughout the building

-Floors being properly sanitized

Schedule a tour today! www.evhigh.com or call (480) 981.2008

Thank you #EVHSSUMMERCREW for all of the hours & hardwork!

Ps, it’s not too late to join our family and especially not too late to think about joining this upcoming fall!

Commitment to Excellence

Our Mission is to provide an educational program that challenges each student, including at-risk students, to attain his or her highest academic and character potential through a coordinated Humanities curriculum, an integrated performing and fine arts program, community service and a traditional approach to education. Come visit our school to see if it’s a good fit for you! #arizonaschool #mesaschool #eastvalleyhighschool #mesaarizona #mesa #arizona #education #traditionalschool

Voting registration Deadline: July 14, 2020 in both Linn and Miami Counties

Miami County: Early voting begins July 15 during regular Business hours. Extended hours until 7:00 p.m. on July 23rd and 30th. Saturdays July 18th, 25th, and August 1st 8:00 a.m to 12:30 p.m.; Monday, August 3rd, 8:00 a.m - Noon

post_text: Congratulations to Lesa Isbell on her retirement from Prairie View USD 362! Lesa served as an elementary teacher at La Cygne Elementary School for 22 years. Her favorite memories of her career include the time where her and her husband hosted her first grade students for an overnight stay at their house, one night for the boys, and the next for the girls. She also mentioned starting with five others at La Cygne Elementary at the same time and Mr. Ferrel taking them under his wing. They had a blast. She wants everyone to know that every teaching partner she had was great and her La Cygne school family is fantastic. She will be missed by fellow staff members and students. Best of luck to Lesa upon her retirement!

post_text: Congratulations to Janice Schubert on her retirement from Prairie USD 362. She has taught for 27 years with the last 13 years as an English Language Arts teacher Prairie View Middle School. When asked what her favorite memory was of teaching these past 27 years she replied, “seeing the light bulb moments when kids understood what they were being taught”. We appreciate everything that Mrs. Schubert has done for the students of Prairie View Middle School. You will be missed by faculty and students. Best of luck to Janice in retirement!

post_text: Congratulations to Connie Nickell on her retirement from Prairie View USD 362! Connie will be missed by her fellow faculty members and her students. Connie retires with 24 years of service at Parker Elementary. She will remember fondly the staff Christmas breakfasts. One of her favorite student memories is the time she screamed in front of the students when she stomped on a spider in PE class and then looked at her shoe and realized it was still on her shoe. Best of luck to Connie in retirement!

Tomorrow, Thursday May 21, will be the last day for food service for the school year. This was our planned last day of school. We have coordinated with the La Cygne library to ensure that meals are still available to our students.
Tomorrow students will receive enough meals to cover until next Tuesday. Starting Tuesday, May 26th the LaCygne library will be doing grab and go meals. All meals handed out tomorrow will have information sheets regarding summer meals. Please check your email for more information.
We are so thankful for the food service, transportation and all the employees that came in to make sure our students got meals on a consistent basis. We are are also thankful for our parents and students that came and got meals each week.
We hope that all our families are well and ready for summer!

Wow- what a different end to a school year for all of us!
We could not pass up Teacher Appreciation Week without saying a huge thank you to each and everyone on the staff of Prairie View USD 362. Each of you play a vital role in the success of our district and success of our students.
To show the appreciation for each of you we have purchased chairs for each member of our staff.
Teachers, aides, paras, secretaries, cooks, custodians, administrators, technology staff, transportation staff, maintenance staff, specialists- it takes everyone to make our school district successful! Staff please check your school email for pick up directions.
Happy Teacher Appreciation Week from USD 362 Board of Education and Prairie View Administrative Team!!
Thanks for all you do!

Art in all its forms is a wonderful social emotional outlet for student creativity. Check out the exhibits over the next few weeks at the Lumberyard Arts Center, and then we invite students and community members to create a new piece of art inspired by the exhibit. Why? Because here in Baldwin City, ART IS CONTAGIOUS!

--

On March 18th, 2020, the Families First Coronavirus Response Act was passed and made changes to SNAP (Food Stamps). See attachments for a complete list of changes and answers to frequently asked questions.

Please share these informational handouts on the changes to SNAP with your clients.

On April 29, the Department of Children and Families (DCF) opened a virtual call center to answer questions about assistance programs including cash, food and child care assistance. Kansans can call 1-888-369-4777.

DCF also added a chat function to its public website. Visitors can use the chat to get basic information about programs and application processes.

Visit http://www.dcf.ks.gov/COVID19/Pages/default.aspx for all DCF program information.

DCF encourages Kansans to monitor the agency’s Facebook and Twitter pages for the latest information about office access and services.

Thank you for your service to the community!

Cierra Smallwood
She/Her/Hers
SNAP-Ed Nutrition Educator
K-State Research and Extension-Douglas County

post_text:
DCF to Distribute Emergency Food Assistance Benefit
Kansans to receive maximum benefit

Governor Laura Kelly and Department for Children and Families Secretary Laura Howard today announced the agency will begin issuing an emergency food assistance benefit to current program households. The emergency allotment increases a household’s monthly benefit to the maximum allowed for the size of the family. The maximum monthly benefit for a family of four is $646.



No application is necessary to receive the emergency funds. Families should start to see their March emergency benefit on their EBT card beginning April 15. The distribution will follow the standard alphabetical schedule. DCF will distribute April emergency benefits in mid to late May.

Households that already receive the maximum monthly allotment are not eligible for emergency assistance. The allotment is for March and April only.

Those interested in applying for food assistance can visit the DCF website at www.dcf.ks.gov. For more information on COVID-19 visit: www.kdheks.gov/coronavirus.

post_text:
Parenting Hotline

Kansas Children's Service League’s Parent Helpline (1-800-CHILDREN) is designed to meet the needs of parents and caregivers who feel isolated and frustrated when trying to meet the challenges of raising children. Information, referrals and support are provided statewide anonymously to parents, relatives, caregivers, youth and professionals 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

The Parent Helpline is also available as a mobile phone App. This is a free download that can be found by searching The Parent Helpline in either the Android or Apple stores. The App also contains links for Parent Tip Cards on the KCSL website or has simple one touch dialing if you decide you need additional information.

Top reasons for calls to the Parent Helpline include basic parenting, child development, behavior management, questions about reporting child abuse and neglect, mental health, legal concerns and information about programs/services. Call the Parent Helpline at either 1-800-CHILDREN

Dear Parents and Guardians

Teachers and other staff have worked hard this week to prepare lessons for students to complete at home next week and through May 19th. Because this is new to all of us, Monday and beyond will not be perfect. Please be patient and we will adjust to challenges. Communicate with your child's teacher regularly either by the video conferencing platform, email or phone. If your internet connection is not strong or you do not have internet, teachers will contact you to deliver assignments to your children in another manner.

Due to missing so many days of school two days that would have been days off will now be learning days. April 10th and 13th will now be learning days. Teachers might not contact students those days but we are counting them as learning days.

The Douglas County Health Department has created a one stop coronavirus information site. You can reach the site through the Lawrence-Douglas County Health Department site or you can go directly to DouglasCountyKs.org/coronavirus.

Have a great weekend and stay healthy!

Just a couple of notes on the meal program that started today. Tomorrow it is set for pickup at the Baldwin Dance and Voice Academy . It is free for children 1 to 18. As we plan for continuation of education we need to know families that do not have internet at home. Please let them know to call so we can work on meeting their needs. Thanks.

This afternoon the Douglas County Health Department Leadership met with all Douglas County Superintendents to discuss possible closures of schools due to the Coronavirus outbreak. At that meeting Douglas County Health Officials informed us that schools in Douglas County will be required to close from now until March 30th. Prior to that date another decision will be made as to whether that closure will need to be extended beyond March 30th. This includes all classes, activities and events. There will be an official press release and announcement later today. They ask that social distancing be observed by all. Students should stay home during this time if at all possible. All large gatherings should not occur. I will send out more specifics later as I know this brings up many questions, but I wanted to get this notice to you as soon as possible so that you can plan for the coming days.
https://ldchealth.org/civicalerts.aspx?AID=234

post_text:
Racial Slur Allegations

During the high school volleyball game Tuesday evening between Baldwin and Piper serious allegations of racial slurs were leveled against students of BHS. This district does not tolerate racist remarks or actions of any kind. During the past few days Baldwin High School Administration have been investigating the allegations in cooperation with the administration of Piper High School. The internal investigation is nearing conclusion. Due to the seriousness of these allegations we have requested an independent outside investigation from the Kansas Association of School Boards legal department. That investigation should begin sometime next week. At this time it would not be appropriate to comment about any findings until all investigations are complete.

Baldwin High School Girls Soccer is celebrating our teammate.
June 27, 2019 at 12:37 PM ·

2019 Graduate Caitlin Countryman has been named by United Soccer Coaches as a Scholar All-American, one of the most prestigious honors awarded by the national coaches organization. Caitlin was one of only three Kansans on the Scholar All-Region team, and the only player selected for the Scholar All-America Team!

To qualify for this award, graduating seniors must have recognized excellence on the field, performed community service locally or nationally, and have maintained a cumulative GPA of at least 3.75. Congratulations, Caitlin! A very deserved honor to cap off four unforgettable years at Baldwin High.

The official United Soccer Coaches announcement with All-Region and All-America teams can be found here: https://unitedsoccercoaches.org/high-school-scholar-all-america-teams-announced/

Longtime Baldwin High School teacher and coach Ginny Honomichl is receiving the honor of a lifetime this week as she is being inducted into the National Federation Hall of Fame in Indianapolis.

post_text:
This school year is nearly complete as the last day for all students will be May 22nd, with a half day of classes. We will serve breakfast that morning, but will not serve lunch. The Primary Center will begin dismissal at about 11:10am. The Intermediate Center will begin dismissal at 11:30am. The Junior High and the High School will dismiss at about 11:30am. Bus routes will run at those times, but since it is not a normal staging of dismissal times the routes may take a little longer than normal.

High School Graduation is this Saturday, May 18th, at 10:00am in the Junior High's gymnasium. Parking lots will fill quickly, so we recommend car pooling and/or have someone drop you off at the event. Seating will also fill quickly, so we recommend arriving early. Doors to the Junior High will open at 8am.

8th grade promotion is scheduled for Monday, May 20th, at 7pm in the Junior High gymnasium.

Have a great end of the school year.

USD 348 Nutrition Services is excited to announce a free, healthy summer meal program for youth ages 1 – 18 starting May 28. The summer meals program is made possible by the USDA’s Summer Food Service Program and several local community partners. Anyone ages 1 - 18 may come to a meal site and eat for free. No sign-up or personal information required. Adults may purchase a meal for $3.75 in advance by contacting Nichole Burnett (785) 594-2721 EXT 120 or nicholeburnett@usd348.com. Find out more here:
http://www.usd348.com/for-parents/food-service/summer-food-service-program/

post_text: Congratulations!
shared_text:
Oak Hills High School
June 2 at 1:03 PM ·

Congratulations to OHHS freshman Allison Rodriquez, whose exhibit “We Can Do It: Women in World War II” advanced to the final round of competition in California’s National History Day - a national research-based project contest. This places her work among the top 15 individual exhibits in the state!

Joining Allison at the “virtual” state competition were senior Karen Palma Garcia with her exhibit “Innovation on the Battlefield of the Civil War,” and senior Angel Moreno Rosales with his documentary “We the People: 229 Years Later” (https://youtu.be/ZiiLPaVGBu0 ). They represented Oak Hills HS and San Bernardino County among over 1,700 students from all over California in the “virtual” National History Day contest.

shared_text:
High Desert Second Chance
March 19 at 9:15 PM ·

High Desert Second Chance in partnership with Victorville Community Service Council is working to answer the needs of our seniors. As we see more and more in the news, our seniors are our most vulnerable as they are unable to cope with the inaccessibility to shop for the basic of necessities, more so now due to the current Covid-19 epidemic.

High Desert Second Chance and Victorville Community Service Council are working together to provide boxes of food supply that will be delivered to our inhome seniors.

Seniors that are in need of this service can call 760-243-9464 to request service. Restrictions may apply.

post_text: With the governor's latest "Safer at Home" order, student meals are continuing to be offered at Cedar Middle School, Hesperia High School, and Sultana High School in a “grab and go” format from 11:00am – 1:00pm Monday through Friday. Students must be present in the vehicle to receive a meal. Even though next week is spring break, we have decided to continue serving meals at the three locations. After spring break, we will determine if we can expand serving locations.

Hesperia Unified Nutrition Services is at Hesperia Unified School District.
March 16 at 7:46 AM · Hesperia, CA ·

Attention Hesperia USD Parents and Students! Nutrition Services is dedicated to providing nutritious meals to students, especially during these difficult times. Between March 16th – 20th, free breakfast and lunch “Grab-and-Go” meals will be available for all children between the ages of 2 – 18 at the following time and locations:
.
Time: 11am – 1pm
.
Locations:
· Hesperia High School (9898 Maple Ave) – Bus Drop Off
· Sultana High School (17311 Sultana St) – Main Parking Lot
· Cedar Middle School (13565 Cedar St) – West Parking Lot
.
For your safety, you will be able to drive up and pick up the meals without leaving your car. Children must be physically present at the time of pickup. @ Hesperia Unified School District

post_text:
Congratulations to Oak Hills team "Dogbyte" for representing HUSD in a nationwide competition called picoCTF. The team placed 197 out of 5,151 active teams. Team "Dogbyte" placed in the top 3.8% of the entire nation.

picoCTF is a free computer security game targeted at middle and high school students, created by security experts at Carnegie Mellon University. The game consists of a series of challenges centered around a unique storyline where participants must reverse engineer, break, hack, decrypt, or do whatever it takes to solve the challenge. The challenges are all set up with the intent of being hacked, making it an excellent, legal way to get hands-on experience. Congratulations to team members Diego Briceno Estrada, Tyler Behrends, Connery Ryan, and Sky Hernandez
Job well done!

#IMPACTtheFutureHUSD #PreparingTodaysStudentsforTomorrowsWorld

post_text: Culinary Arts students from Hesperia High School and Sultana High School were invited to gain real world experience by preparing a meal and providing table serve at the Top Chamber Business Awards Ceremony. The event was hosted by Assemblyman Jay Obernolte on Thursday, October 17, in Apple Valley. Led by Culinary Arts instructors Chris Pisciotti, Sherry Jones and Rick Cabrera, Culinary Arts students gained valuable experience while serving over 150 local business representatives, government officials and guests. Students practiced the 4C's of Communication, Critical Thinking, Creativity, and Collaboration as they represented Hesperia Unified School District.

Today, May 21, from 2:00 p.m -5:00 p.m. we will have drive-up pick up at the athletic entrance of the high school. Please stay in you cars and someone will assist you.

shared_text:
Mt. Pleasant Area Community Foundation
April 30 at 3:00 PM ·

🎓💵The 2020 scholarship recipients have been selected! The Mt. Pleasant Area Community Foundation is awarding 106 scholarships, totaling $120,100, to local students.

We'd like to extend a special thank you to our board of trustees and the 95 volunteers on the scholarship committee who, as a result of the stay-at-home executive order, adjusted to using new tools to continue reviewing scholarship applications so that the process could continue seamlessly.

Congratulations students and thank you to all of our applicants! We wish you much success as you continue your education!

For a list of scholarships and the recipients, visit: www.mpacf.org/news/community-foundation-awards-120100-in-scholarships-to-local-students

Thanks for posting it, MPHS!
shared_text:
Mount Pleasant High School
April 3 at 10:08 AM ·

Class of 2020 Seniors

Please know that MPHS will continue to support you as you begin to enter the next phase in life! The Counseling Office wants to share this FREE online series sponsored by MSU Extension and Mid Michigan College called Adulting 101. This 4-part series covers some very practical instruction and tips on things like managing your personal finances, what to know about renting your own place, and all that goes with living on your own. Please refer to the flyer for dates/times and how to register/login.
Meeting password: Adult101

Stay engaged and keep investing in yourselves!

post_text:
Good morning, Oilers!

If you have not already done so, please check your email for a letter from me! Although our buildings are now closed for the remainder of the year, school is not over! MPPS teachers and administrators are working together to develop a plan for our students. This plan will include continued learning for every student.

In the coming weeks, we will share our plan for how we will meet the academic, social and emotional needs of all students.

Once the "Stay Home, Stay Safe" order has been lifted, we will also have a plan for families to pick up personal items (locker and desk supplies, instruments, etc.).

Thank you for your patience as we put our plans together!

Stay strong and healthy, Oilers!
-Mrs. Verleger

💙Yesterday was an amazing day for our school lunch program! We served 940 people — that’s 940 lunches AND 940 breakfasts!!

💛We also passed out hundreds of academic enrichment packets for students in grades DK -8th! Wow!

💙Thank you to all of our staff and volunteers who made this happen!

💛We are ready to go again today with lunch and breakfast! We have also restocked our academic enrichment packets in case you didn’t get one yesterday!

💙We would love to see you all again today!

💛Oilers, we are stronger together!
#OP

post_text:
Thank you to everyone who helped out today!

Just today, we delivered 668 meals — breakfast and lunch — plus bags from the Care Store and the Community Compassion Network!!

That makes our total for the week 2398 meals!! Wow!! What an amazing gift to the children of our community! We cannot do it alone!!

Thank you to our community partners and supporters — McDonalds, Frito Lay, Michigan Dairy Producers, CCN and the Care Store!! This is a perfect example of the power of collaboration in our community!

Also thank you to the numerous volunteers who helped this week — MPPS teachers, administrators, support staff members, bus drivers, food service staff, community volunteers, parents....the list goes on and on!!

Thank you!! We are stronger together!! See you next week!!
💙💛💙💛

Oiler Pride!!!

post_text:
MP Families -- We will be sending out additional information to you later today in an attempt to answer your questions regarding the extended closure due to Governor Whitmer's order to close all K-12 schools for the next three weeks.

For now, please know that we are asking students to take home their personal belongings at the end of the day today. This will help our custodial team with disinfecting our buildings. Students will not be able to enter our schools after 6:00 p.m. today. If your child has medications or other essential items that they need stored in school, please contact the office to make arrangements to pick them up before the end of the regular school day.

Thank you for your support as we work through this unique time for our schools and community! Be well!
#OP

Parents -- please check on your children and make sure they have the supports they need. We are here for you. Contact your child's school if you need additional assistance.

The Rotary Beef Dinner will be held this year on February 21st. The beef dinner is a great meal and a nice opportunity to support all that Rotary does for our community.

The Mount Pleasant Rotary has multiple programs that support MPHS. (We have our own High School level Rotary club - Interact). The Rotary sponsors our Citizen of the Month Award and grants multiple scholarships through the Citizen of the month program. The Rotary also awards scholarships through the Community Foundation.

post_text:
January is School Board Recognition Month. This is a time to recognize the individuals who approach their volunteer work like it is a full-time job and with extraordinary dedication to public schools. They are citizens whose decisions affect our children and build our community.

Through their dedication, governance, advocacy and collaboration with other school district staff, they are building the future of education in Michigan.

This month, we encourage all members of the community to thank a board member. Please take this opportunity to show your appreciation for these servant leaders and begin to better understand how local trustees work together to prepare today’s students to be tomorrow’s leaders.

We are grateful for your service and dedication to our district and community!

Thank you!!
#OP

November 6, 2019 at 11:08 AM · Mount Pleasant, MI ·

Today, ICCU surprised our first teacher who was awarded with the ICCU Mini Grant! Congratulations, Robin Ervin at Vowles Elementary! Ms. Ervin requested a new rug for her 1st grade students to sit on for reading and other classroom activities, it even goes along with her classroom theme of "The Busy Bees". We hope your class enjoys your new rug for several years to come! Thank you for all you do for your students!

post_text: Fall Practice…..The junior high and high school Fall sports seasons officially begin on Monday, August 17. All participants are expected to be at practice, times are listed below. Thornton Graphics
March 19 at 3:37 PM ·

CONTEST TIME! Our amazing Bullpup school staff is working hard to see that, through this challenging time, every child is fed two meals a day. We will be making signs for the delivery vehicles and this worthy project NEEDS A NAME! What should it be called? We are running a contest through Tuesday, March 24. Tell us in the comments of THIS ORIGINAL POST what you think we should name it. The winner will be chosen Wednesday, March 25 and will receive a $20 gift certificate to Thornton Graphics! To ensure your entry is counted, you MUST comment with your suggestion on the original post on OUR PAGE.

post_text:
CVHS is excited to announce that Erin RItter has been accepted into Washington University's Mclevey School of Engineering, where she will be studying biomedical engineering in the fall!

Erin Ritter said, "This process was long and arduous. Beginning the summer before junior year, this required several essays, recommendation letters, and countless of standardized tests. On December 2nd, I was notified of my acceptance into Washington University with a full-ride scholarship through the Questbridge National College Match! I am sincerely grateful for the support I have received from family and faculty at CVHS throughout my highschool career. Without this, and the generosity of Questbridge and WashU, none of this would have been possible."

On December 2nd, I was notified of my acceptance into Washington University with a full-ride scholarship through the Questbridge National College Match! post_text:
Remember- Spring Skiing starts tomorrow! 5th-8th graders riding the bus, please be loaded and ready to go by 7:15 A.M.

All other participants should also plan to depart the school close to the same time. PLEASE MAKE SURE YOU HAVE PRE-ARRANGED TRANSPORTATION. Do not drop your child off unattended and assume someone will take them. We need to check in at Pomerelle as a group, and you will not receive your lift ticket until the bus has arrived. If you are joining us, you are required to participate in the lesson programs (all students & adults). Please do not ditch. This is why we receive the discounted pricing.

If you are renting, you will need to take payment with you ($10 for skiing, $15 for boarders. Helmets are an additional $15 and strongly encouraged). Please bring a lunch or extra money for food at the lodge. Dress in layers. Sunscreen and sunglasses/goggles are also strongly encouraged.

If you have any questions, please contact the school at (208)548-2221. Thank you!

http://www.pomerelle.com/conditions/index.htm
