post_text:
Good morning!

Thank you to everyone who participated in our Spring Ski Survey. Our officials dates will be:

March 13th
March 20th
March 27th
April 3rd

Forms will be sent home with 5-8th grade students. Additional forms will be available at the office or on our website.

Once again, we need chaperones to ride the bus with the 5-8th grade students. This is limited to 5 individuals each week. First come, first serve, and you are rewarded with a free lift ticket for the day! Please let us know if you are interested.

Finally, many of you may have heard of the unfortunate accident that took place at Pomerelle earlier this month when a young man died from injuries sustained after colliding with a tree. Helmets are strongly recommended for participants, and can be rented at the Pomerelle Ski shop for $15. Quantity is limited at the shop, so you may want to consider purchasing your own as well. Here is a great article to help you make your selection:
shared_text:
OUTDOORGEARLAB.COM
How to Choose a Ski or Snowboard Helmet | OutdoorGearLab

post_text:
If approved by voters March 3, the new North Scott Community YMCA would offer a six-lane competition pool, a zero-depth entry pool, a full-size basketball court, a complete fitness area, a walking track, locker rooms, a workout room, and child watch.

There would also be opportunities for the school district swim team and aquatic therapy for students with special needs, senior citizens and athletes. The Y would offer lessons to enhance swimming safety, as well.

In addition, there would be YMCA scholarships available for low-income residents. There would also be community meeting spaces built into the design.

Learn more: https://www.northscottymca.org

post_text:
Hardin County Fair Royalty

Lance Good will be representing Hardin Northern as the 2020 Hardin County Fair Royalty King contestant. Lance is the son of Brent and Jennie Good of Kenton. Lance is a very active student within the school district. He participates in Football, Basketball, Baseball, Track & Field. He has served two terms as the Hardin Northern FFA president and is also an officer for the History Brigade club. Lance is a member of the Hardin County Junior Fairboard and exhibits market goats at the county fair.

Maddie Grappy is Hardin Northern's Queen representative for the Hardin County Fair royalty contest. Maddie is the daughter of Josh and Denise Grappy of Dola. She has been an FFA officer for three years and has also played basketball and softball throughout her high school career. She is a member of the National Honor Society, Student Council, and has been on the Junior Fairboard for two years.

Thursday 8.27 Athletic Schedule:

4:30 pm - JH volleyball hosts Riverdale.
6:00 pm - HS volleyball hosts USV.

All Tickets are $5. Both games will be played in the HS gym.
Gym will be cleared after the JH game and a new ticket will be required for entry. HS tickets must be purchased by the player prior to the end of the school day. JH tickets may be purchased during the school day or at the game. Each player is entitled to buy 4 tickets for their game.

Both events will be livestreamed here on the HN page.

Volleyball players may also purchase their tickets for Saturday's games. These tickets need to be purchased by the end of the school day Friday. JH hosts Riverside at 9:30 am and HS hosts Delphos Jefferson at 11 am. Gym will be cleared between games and a ticket would be needed for each game.

We are in the process of setting up online ticketing through Hometown Ticketing. We hope to have this up and running by next week's games. All tickets will then be available for purchase online!

We appreciate your patience as we work through these difficult times.

post_text: HN Community: Please beware of Facebook live stream event posts claiming to live stream our athletic events. These are phishing scams attempting to lure you to their website to pay a subscription fee and to collect your credit card information. If a game is live streamed we will have more information about the event on our FB page and/or our website. These scam events will display the page name on the event page. See image.

post_text: Update on the ticket policy for the upcoming high school volleyball games. Each volleyball player may purchase four tickets. These tickets must be purchased by the end of the school day of the scheduled contest. There will be no tickets sold at the gate. Spectators must wear a mask and sit on marked seats. Only families from the same household may sit together. The high school volleyball team will host Arcadia tomorrow at 5:30 p.m., Upper Scioto Valley on Thursday at 6:00 pm, and Delphos Jefferson on Saturday at 11:00 a.m. The junior high volleyball team will host Riverdale on Thursday at 4:30 p.m. and Riverside on Saturday at 9:30 a.m. All games will be played in the high school gym. The gym will be cleared after each junior high game and a new ticket will be needed for entry into the high school game. Due to a league policy change, beginning with Thursday’s games all tickets will be $5. Information on these events and upcoming ticket policies will be posted on the school websites and updated with this messaging system as soon as it is available.

post_text: Athletes entering grades 7-12 next school year are reminded to check their email for a survey about summer workouts if they have not done so yet and to look for information from each sport's coach. Any athlete participating in a summer workout must bring a signed Return to Play form which is in the email or posted on the HN website to the first workout. Athletes must follow these guidelines while working out, should self-check for symptoms prior to arriving and report any symptoms to their coach upon arrival. Athletes will be required to maintain social distancing at all times. If you feel sick or have a borderline temperature please stay home. We are excited about the possibility of returning to sports but there is still risk and we all must take precautions for the health and safety of all involved. Thank you in advance for your cooperation.

post_text: The Hardin Northern FFA students assisted with Keep Hardin County Beautiful on May 12th. They helped with putting new soil into the 22 earth pots around the courthouse. The students unloaded 60 bags of soil and filled the pots in under an hour. Deb Oestreich, program manger of Keep Hardin Northern Beautiful, said our students "were such great kids, so willing to help and followed instructions and kept asking what else they could do to assist". Awesome job to the following students; Wes Newton, Kylee Legge, Brenna Flowers, Cadence Coy, Alyssa Heldman, Aliya Obenour, Renee Newton, Alex Stewart and Seth Parsons!

post_text: On Tuesday, Mrs. Mast Class learned about the difference between the Artic and Antarctic. Then they explored the Artic region. They learned that the Artic is mostly water surrounded by land up at the North Pole. They also learned that ground in the Artic is permanently frozen but stuff can still grow. Some animals they saw included trumpeter swan, wolverines, arctic fox, humpback whales, seals, walrus, and snowy owl. Lastly they saw a polar bear and learned that they are great swimmers!

Good afternoon everyone. I am writing to let you know that any 7-12 student that has completed their requirements for the year can start dropping off their materials on Monday, May 18, 2020, at the front of the building.

Starting Monday, designated areas will be located along the front porch of the school. Each student needs to place the correct materials in the correct designated area. For example, 7th grade math will have a separate area than 7th grade science, 7th grade history, and 7th grade ELA. Also, please make sure to have your name located in all textbooks so that the materials can be documented.

Material collection will run from 7:00 am to 6:00 pm Monday through Friday of next week.

***CHROMEBOOKS WILL NOT BE COLLECTED***--Students will be permitted to keep their Chromebooks over the summer and are expected to keep them in good, running shape. If you have any issues with your chromebook, please contact Mr. Emerine.

Have a good evening and GO POLAR BEARS!

post_text: Welcome to our virtual Polar Bear Showcase! By going to this link (http://hn.k12.oh.us/district/schools/pbl/) you will find projects that were made by Hardin Northern students this year. Our students demonstrated their knowledge and skills by creating a public product or presentation for a real audience. This is called Project Based Learning and this helps students learn by actively engaging them in real-world problems or answering complex questions. If you visit the website above, one of the projects you will find is "How can we as musicians use our knowledge of rhythms to create a piece of music using found sounds or homemade instruments?". Students created a “Sound Score” using found sounds, including sounds from objects such as basketballs, deer antlers, lockers, hubcaps, and boots. They worked in groups to find their own instruments, notate a score, and perform their score. Below you can find the some photos from this project. We encourage you to visit the website above and check out all the great work the students did this past year

post_text: Welcome to our virtual Polar Bear Showcase! By going to this link (http://hn.k12.oh.us/district/schools/pbl/) you will find projects that were made by Hardin Northern students this year. Our students demonstrated their knowledge and skills by creating a public product or presentation for a real audience. This is called Project Based Learning and this helps students learn by actively engaging them in real-world problems or answering complex questions. If you visit the website above, one of the projects you will find is "How can we, as first graders, teach others about the importance of saving bees?". First grade students researched bees and developed this question to drive their project: How can we design a plan to bring awareness of the limited number of bees? They learned about bee life cycles, the different “jobs” of bees, parts of bees, bee habitats, and dangers to bees, then began working on a plan to teach others what they had learned. We encourage you to visit the website above and check out all the great work the students did this past year

Senior Brayden Flowers, son of Eric and Doreen Flowers is pictured here completing one of his complete at home food labs for his Principles of Nutrition and Wellness class at Hardin Northern. post_text: HN Students and Families... if you need books, please reach out to us and we can prepare bags for pick up for K-12 students with lunch/breakfast or with K-3 materials provided by teachers for pick up. Email Mrs. Wilson (darlene.wilson@hardinnorthern.org) to request books. We will not be concerned with due dates or returns at this point. We just would like to provide reading materials to our students! In addition, all of our students in grades 7-12 have access to free digital books and resources through the HN Public Library (hardinnorthernpl.org). They each have a card with necessary information for logging in. If they have misplaced it, they can email me for their username and pin number.

FEATURES

Spectrum makes it simple for qualified households to sign up for low income Internet assistance.

- Free Internet modem
- High-speed Internet at 30 Mbps
- No data caps
- No contracts

REQUIREMENTS

- Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) of the NSLP
- Supplemental Security Income (for applicants age 65+ only)

post_text: Hardin Northern High School is doing scheduling differently this year. Students received the course description book and master schedule in February. Copies can be found at hardinnorthern.org/guidance. Students are to figure out what class they are taking for each of the 8 periods and be prepared to sign up for those classes on the following nights: Current Juniors at 7:30 pm and current sophomores at 8:30 pm on Monday, March 9 in the varsity gym. Current freshman at 7:30 pm and current 8th graders at 8:30 pm on Wednesday March 11 in the varsity gym. Parents are asked to accompany students. Students that can’t make it at these times will sign up during GRIT period on the following day. Courses signed up for should be considered final, so make sure students are prepared. post_text:
OEHS Students & Parents,
The high school does not have a specific supply list. We do recommend your student come prepared with writing utensils and notebook for hand note-taking, The Chromebooks they will be given have a calculator built-in, but if they prefer handhelds, it is suggested to get a scientific calculator. The best calculator for upper math level courses, Algebra II, Pre-Calculus or Calculus is TI-84. However, any scientific calculator will be sufficient as the TI-84 is expensive. The school is not providing planners this year, as the handbook will be online. Students would benefit from a daily planner and a binder as a way to stay organized. Students can use pocket folders instead of a binder, but they should have something to help them stay organized.

post_text:
E. E. Knight will be doing a school-wide rock garden/path project paid for by a grant written and received by Mrs. Travis.
The purpose of the project is to have students leave a "footprint" at the school. The positive message in the book Only One You by Linda Kranz is "There is only one you in this great big world...make it a better place." All students will read the book and take part in a discussion of the messages and wisdom shared. Each student will then paint their own "fish" rock and put their name underneath.
All of the completed rocks will be put together in a path in front of EEK and each year the path will grow.
Please search for the perfect rock with your child and bring to school the first week of school.

post_text: It was great to see all the seniors the last couple days! 340 Miles, 14 hours, and a broken GPS system couldn't keep us from seeing you guys push pause on Fortnite, Snapchat, and your boredom! We weren't able to take pictures with everyone because somehow half of you have jobs that are essential during a pandemic, but for those that had your signs posted, we just took a picture with those! Hope your letters were good reads! Thanks Mr. Kingsbury for making that memory! Thank you to Ms. Blank and all the parents who put the gift bags together, we only dropped one bag, and it blew up the root beer (Gavenda)! Class of 2020- Making Memories!

It was great to see all the seniors the last couple days! 340 Miles, 14 hours, and a broken GPS system couldn't keep us from seeing you guys push pause on Fortnite, Snapchat, and your boredom! We weren't able to take pictures with everyone because somehow half of you have jobs that are essential during a pandemic, but for those that had your signs posted, we just took a picture with those! Hope your letters were good reads! Thanks Mr. Kingsbury for making that memory! Thank you to Ms. Blank and all the parents who put the gift bags together, we only dropped one bag, and it blew up the root beer (Gavenda)! Class of 2020- Making Memories!

post_text:
OEHS Parents and OE Community Members,

The advisory council is requesting your input and assistance with planning advisory events, advisory lessons, lunch groups, and more this upcoming school year! If you are interested in providing input, you can find the Google form by using this link https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdYAfaFQqOLaSKsh2NXhvDL_xxWqx87s4t-eqhHQLkUolrxQQ/viewform?usp=sf_link

Please be aware that all events or lessons concerning sensitive topics as well as any lunch groups will be discussed with parents via a parent letter that will be sent home requesting permission for your child/children to participate. We also hope to provide an advisory calendar that will be located on our Student Services portion of the website as we are closer to the 2020-2021 school year. This advisory calendar is subject to change. So, please refer to it often if you have any questions regarding what lesson will be taught or what event will be held.

post_text:
All OEHS students who turned in a dual enrollment approval form should have received an email from Mrs. Ehman asking them to confirm their LCC course selections (unless they had previously emailed her those). She is now in the process of double checking placement scores to determine if your child needs to take the Accuplacer test or not. Your child should receive an email from her indicating that their placement scores were sufficient or not and what their next steps are. If your child does not receive an email from her, it is likely that they have not turned in their new dual enrollment approval form AND/OR they have not confirmed their LCC course selections. It is imperative that they do both of these before they can move forward in the dual enrollment process.

For a detailed explanation of the LCC process and other Student Services updates, please visit this link https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1wgPQGw7_ZdUHPGiA8OvkckiEZVQer0h_?usp=sharing

Dear PARENT/GUARDIAN,

We know these are difficult and unprecedented times. However, at some point, normalcy will return, and memories will be shared. If you have not already ordered the 2020 yearbook, please consider purchasing now at jostens.com.
When you get to the checkout page, you can spread the cost of the yearbook over three monthly payments. We are printing a set amount of yearbooks and when we have reached our limit, the ordering site will stop accepting orders, even if it is before the projected deadline in the summer. Since copies are limited and school schedules continue to change, we ask that you please order your yearbook online.

Stay healthy and safe,

Michael Puffpaff, yearbook advisor
michael.puffpaff@ovidelsie.org

post_text: Congratulations to this busy gal, Maddie G! She was OEHS and 92.5 FM the Castle's Student of the Week LAST week but this is the first chance we'd had to recognize her with attending the FFA State Convention and Robotics Competition last week. Maddie, a senior, is actively involved in FFA public speaking events at both the district and regional levels. She's competed in a Veterinary Science Career Development Event through the Michigan FFA Association, where her team placed in the top 10 out of 300+ teams. Maddie's attended the National FFA Organization’s Washington Leadership Conference in Washington, DC and was awarded the Michigan FFA Alumni sponsorship to attend this event. Not only is Maddie the Vice President of the Ovid-Elsie FFA she's also the VP for NHS and the co-president of her class. Besides being an exceptional student, Maddie is also a member of the Robotics and Golf teams. Her future goals are to attend MSU to study veterinary medicine.

post_text: OEHS is proud to announce 92.5, The Castle's Student of the Week Emma F. Emma, a sophomore, was nominated by Ms. Hoffman and Mrs. Keck because of her kindness to others, dedication, and attentiveness in the classroom. She is always the first to volunteer to help and demonstrates so much enthusiasm. She is a very bright, hardworking and polite person that anyone can enjoy being around. Emma is currently serving as Junior Chapter Secretary and has participated in multiple leadership and skills contests for FFA. She is very active in her dairy 4-H club for Clinton County and is also a member of the Varsity Tennis & JV Bowling teams. Emma has received the most dedicated player award in tennis and received multiple first and second place ribbons in 4-H for various crafts, dairy management, and dairy showmanship. She plans to pursue a degree in agriculture and is undetermined on the specifics.

Ovid-Elsie High School and Middle School would like to thank Coach Jeff Olson from Ishpeming, and the entire Do It For Daniel team for sharing their story.

Friday, Jeff Olson from Do It for Daniel (www.doitfordaniel.com) spoke to our middle and high school students concerning suicide awareness and prevention. Community Mental Health and several county schools provided additional Counseling staff and were available for our students throughout the day. Thank you to our supporting schools.

If your child needs assistance in the coming days, please email Mrs. Ehman or Mrs. Spicer or feel free to call them at 989-834-2271 ext. 1901 and 1421, respectively, or feel free to reach out to any of the service providers on the attached documents by clicking the Google drive link below. In addition, you can call the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 1-800-273-8255 which is available 24 hours a day.

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ce6JmoGVePPnDt2XBHPFTt494x8qfB30?usp=sharing

Team JC
January 18 at 12:19 PM ·

Wow, where to begin with last night’s exciting Coaches vs Cancer games... 🎗🏀
Both OE and New Lothrop girls and boys teams played their hearts out!
60 Seconds of Change was fun to watch and amazing to hear the results. I can’t recall the exact amounts but New Lothrop raised over $1500 and OE raised over $1600 for Coaches vs Cancer...in just 60 seconds!!!
Also amazing, 45 people visited Courtney and her crew at the Be The Match table and registered themselves to be added to the bone marrow donor registry. 45 people! Thank you!! 🧡
The orange in the stands is always inspiring and overwhelming. And wow, our student section really knows how to show love for their fellow student.
#NoOneFightsAlone
#JCStrong
#TeamJC
~ Sarah

post_text: To kick off our holiday break, OEHS students participated in a holiday breakfast this morning. Students were served breakfast while the OEHS band played and were able to pick from several activities including a movie, coloring station, open gyms (basketball, volleyball, etc) and weight room, a euchre tournament, bowling, and much more! We also recognized our November and December Students of the Month. Those students received an extra bag of goodies for showing Marauder PRIDE! Students, we hope you had a great time celebrating the end of exams and the beginning of your break! We hope you have a safe and happy holiday, and we will see you all in the New Year!

post_text: The EEK Library sponsored a Gingerbread House Decorating Contest this year. All students were encouraged to build a gingerbread house at home using cardboard, paper, buttons, cotton balls, and any other non-food items. We ended up with 68 creative and amazing entries! All of the houses have been on display in the library for the past week and students and staff have had the opportunity to vote for their favorites. The top 6 vote-getters were Caroline F., Drake B., Madison H., Logan S., Emberlyn K., and River M. Congratulations to all of these students! Thank you to all who entered and voted!

post_text:
I hope this message finds everyone well and having fun this summer!

I would like all our Big Stone City School families to know that our school has formed a committee and has been meeting regularly for quite some time now on the topic of how we are going to start school this fall. The committee has been working diligently on sifting through all the options for starting this fall and making sure that we have covered all aspects. With all of the different scenarios that could happen and each one having their own pros and cons, we will take the information we are given and make the best decision we can at that time.

The plan will be to have full detailed information at our school registration day, August 4, if not sooner. There has been some talk that in mid-July there will be more substantial guidance given by the state to help in our decision making.

post_text:
I would like to tell you about Kadence Rausch-Frank 8th grade student from Big Stone City School and how she received an award from the Junior Scholastic magazine.

Each year at Big Stone City School, middle school students are entered in a contest to draw and decorate a map from a country chosen by the Junior Scholastic Magazine. The students have to use clues to figure out what country the "Mapman" has visited. They then need to draw a map and decorate it with things that are meaningful to that country. This year, the country was Sri Lanka. There was one national winner and 25 runners up from all over America. Kadence was a runner up and will receive recognition in the magazine and a "Mapman" t-shirt. We are proud of the nice job she did!

Big Stone City School District #25-1
May 12 at 2:30 PM ·

2020 8th Grade Graduation

On May 20th, at 7:00 pm on the front lawn of the Big Stone City School, there will be an 8th grade promotion and awards ceremony. This ceremony will be for the parents, siblings and a limited number of guests invited by each family. The awards ceremony will not be for our entire school. Our intentions are to also live stream this ceremony on Facebook Live for those of you who are interested in viewing.

If you are able and would like to, we ask that you decorate your yard in purple and white to show not only your school spirit but also your support to the 8th grade class as they have had the most unusual 8th grade year and graduation time. Businesses, if you would like to, please decorate the front of your business too. Let's show these students that we care!

Students in the lower grades receiving awards will be picking them up on May 18th when they do their final drop off.

Students in the lower grades receiving awards will be picking them up on May 18th when they do their final drop off.

post_text:
The Big Stone City School's child care center, the Little Lions’ Learning Center, received a donation of $10,000 from Grant County Christian Service Council. The Little Lions’ Learning Center provides child care to 30 children and 45% of these children receive some sort of state or federal assistance. The center employs 8 staff members and is currently at full capacity. They are a non-profit organization that is licensed by the state of South Dakota and participates in the Child and Adult Nutrition Program.

Pictured: Christopher Folk (CEO of Big Stone City School, Anne Lester (Director of Little Lion's Learning Center), and Linda Comstock (Vice President of the Grant County Christian Service Council)

post_text: Recess and outside expectations: We will be following the following procedure for outside PE, recess and before/after school time. If it feels like 45 or less, students are expected to have a sweatshirt, sweater or coat. If it feels like 30 or less, students are expected to have a coat, hat or gloves. If snow is on the ground, they must have snow pants and boots. If it feels like 0 or less, we will be inside. Naturally, conditions in the Fall and Spring can change quickly, so make sure your child is prepared by leaving some extra outside clothes at school. Our goal is to enjoy the time outside, but also understand that 30 degrees is chilly and can be uncomfortable.

On September 20th the students received their first ROAR goal. An adventure hike through space with Chris Roberts, a Big Stone City School Alumni and 2001 graduate of Ortonville. Chris currently works at NASA and is an engineer for a portion of the International Space Station. Our students were able to learn about Chris's education, his job at NASA, some other space facts and were also able to ask him questions.

The students enjoyed this surprise visitor with Big Stone City roots.

Thank you Chris for coming and sharing your story with us!!

On Thursday, August 27, 2020 the Mediapolis School District will start classes. The elementary, grades K-5, will return to school at Level II mitigation and will be coming to school for daily face-to-face instruction with their teachers. The secondary building, grades 6-12, will return at Level III mitigation. Level III calls for a hybrid learning structure. At this time, bus routes are not changing. You will be getting more information regarding returning to school if your child is in the secondary building.

Please continue to monitor communication from the school, we will notify everyone if there are any changes.

post_text: We have continued to monitor the Des Moines County 14 day positivity rate for COVID-19 and continued conversations with Des Moines County Public Health officials. The positivity rate has continued to increase in Des Moines County and we have recently become aware of several Mediapolis students who have been exposed to or have tested positive for COVID-19. Therefore, for the safety of our students, staff, and community the Mediapolis Community School District is delaying the beginning of the 2020-2021 school year until Thursday, August 27. We will have additional information next week about what the start of school will look like for your child.

Mediapolis Baseball/Softball Guidelines

Seating:
Bleacher seating will be limited.
Fans are encouraged to bring their own lawn chairs.

Concessions:
No concession stands due to Iowa Department of Education and Department of Public Health guidelines.
Coolers will be allowed to be brought in and are subject to search.

Restrooms:
Restrooms will be available and cleaned regularly.

Personal Protection Equipment:
Face-masks are not required, but are encouraged.
Spectators are encouraged to bring their own hand sanitizer.

Spectator Expectations:
Accept personal responsibility for following public health guidelines.
Practice safe physical distancing.
Do not use or bring sunflower seeds.
Abide by posted signage.

Admission will be $5 for all fans. School passes are accepted.

As we get started with the season on June 15th, we want you to know that we are doing everything we can to put the health of all players, coaches, and fans as our number one priority.

Rick Whitaker Jeff Martin William Azinger Mediapolis Bullettes Softball Mediapolis Baseball Jaci Steffener Amanda Richardson

post_text:
We feel it is important to make a decision on the 2020 Prom now, so that students and parents have an opportunity to make appropriate plans concerning their schedules and rental/purchasing decisions.

After consultation with Des Moines County Public Health and a review of the governor’s latest proclamation which states that for mass gatherings “The gathering organizer must ensure at least six feet of distance between each group or individual attending alone.” It was determined that six foot social distancing is not feasible in a dance setting.

Therefore, with the health and safety of our students at the forefront of our thoughts and concerns, we are sad to announce that the 2020 Mediapolis High School Prom is cancelled.

post_text:
As you may know, Governor Reynolds announced yesterday that baseball and softball may begin June 1.

Today, superintendents across the state received further guidance from the Iowa Department of Education. We were informed that junior high sports will remain suspended through June 30. It’s important to note that junior high sports are not affiliated with the Iowa High School Athletic Association or the Iowa Girls High School Athletic Union.

As a result of this guidance, the Southeast Iowa Super Conference is canceling junior high baseball and softball for the 2020 summer season. Per IHSAA & IGHSAU rules, eighth-grade students are eligible to play high school baseball and softball if they elect to do so. Contact Activity Director Mike Wolter (wolterm@mepoedu.org) with questions.

The Mediapolis Secondary and Elementary School doors will be open from 7:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. on Tuesday, March 17 for students to gather personal items, musical instruments, and reading books from the school if they choose to. This will be the only day that the school is open to students during the closure. During that time, students are to gather their belongings and then exit the building. This is not a time to hang around and socialize.

Please be reminded that the school closure affects all school programming and all school activities are cancelled during the closure, including before- and after-school activities, all athletic and extracurricular practices, meetings, competitions, and all weekend events.

We will provide additional information as we receive guidance from the governor's office, the Department of Public Health, and the Department of Education.

post_text:
Spring always brings an upswing in the need for volunteers and chaperones (#fieldtrips).

Remember, there are two steps to becoming a volunteer/chaperone in the Mediapolis School District.

Step 1) Complete our Background Check: Authorization for Release of Child & Adult Abuse Information. This step is done ONCE every 5 years for the district and can take several weeks to complete.

Step 2) Complete our Annual Volunteer Acknowledgement Google Form. This step is required upon receiving favorable results from the background check AND annually thereafter.

Volunteers/Chaperones CANNOT volunteer or chaperone until they have completed both steps.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact Trish Borrison, Jana Morian, or Amanda Frank.

post_text:
Please remember that washing hands, covering coughs/sneezes, and staying out of public places when ill (or when others are ill) is essential to preventing the spread of germs. Your children's health (and the health of other children) is most important despite concerns about attendance. For this reason, we ask you to please:

**Keep your child home if he/she has a fever of over 100.0. A fever indicates that your child has an infection that could be passed to other students. If you give your child Motrin or Tylenol, the fever will, of course, disappear but he/she will still be contagious to others. Children must be fever-free for 24 hours (without medication) before returning to school!

**Call your school nurse if you are concerned about truancy issues.
Thank you!”

Kelli Petersen, RN
Mediapolis School Nurse
petersenk@mepoedu.org
319-394-3101 Ext. 1413
319-394-9753 Fax

CONSIDER RUNNING FOR YOUR SCHOOL BOARD!

MEDIAPOLIS SCHOOL BOARD ELECTION TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2019

Iowa School Board elections now take place in November of odd-numbered years (previously elections were in September). Mediapolis School will have four open positions on the November 5, 2019 ballot. The length of each term is four years. Please note that Mediapolis School no longer has “director districts” and that all school board positions are “at large”.

Nomination Papers may be taken out at the Mediapolis School Superintendent’s Office August 26--September 19, 2019. For more information, contact Dawn Kelly, Mediapolis School Board Secretary, at kellyd@mepoedu.org or 319 394-3101 ext 1452.

Anyone interested in becoming a board member should also consider attending upcoming board meetings. Our next board meeting will be held on August 12, 2019 at 6:30 PM in the administration office board room.

post_text:
This has certainly been an unusual year with winter weather! Students have missed 10 days of school. In my 32 years as an educator, I remember only one other time we missed this much school due to weather.

When we built our 2018-2019 school calendar we included 2 snow makeup days, but having 8 additional days to makeup has created significant challenges for students, families, and school staff. After a great deal of thought, weighing options, many conversations with parents, board members and teachers, we have developed a revised school schedule that will allow us to make up the missed instructional time and have students complete the school year on May 24th. Please use the Link below to see a revised schedule for the remaining portion of the 2018-2019 school year.

Please notice starting March 4th we will be adding 30 minutes to the end of each school day.

http://www.meposchools.org/wp-content/files/2019/02/Snow-Day-Makeup-Plan.pdf

post_text: The Mediapolis Fine Arts Boosters are hosting a “Marvel Madness Lock-In” on February 8/9 for students in grades 5-8 at Mediapolis High School. Additional information and permission forms can be found at http://www.meposchools.org/carol-keller-hein/fine-arts-booster-fundraiser or contact Mrs. Keller-Hein at 319-394-3101, ext. 1120, keller-heinc@mepoedu.org.

The threat was taken very seriously and procedures have been taken to prioritize the safety of the students and staff in our school.

We apologize for the anxiety that this situation puts on the students, staff and community in our school district. We strive to keep the safety and security of our students and staff as our highest priority.

post_text:
Iowa Statewide Assessment of Student Progress (ISASP)

ISASP will replace the Iowa Assessments as Iowa's main state accountability test for all students in the spring of 2019.

Notable changes between the Iowa CAP and Iowa Assessments:

Includes a language arts and writing assessment
Science is only offered for grades 5, 8, & 10
Online administration availablity
Some sections of the test will not be timed
Longer administration time (4 hours for reading, language/writing, and mathematics and an additional 45 minutes for science

For families who want their kids to have an advantage on State Assessments this year, Practice Tests are available at the following link.

http://iowa.pearsonaccessnext.com/test-prep/

Recognizing Outstanding Achievement:

Hopkins Middle School would like to recognize Katie Taylor for her outstanding achievement throughout her three years here. Not only was she an All A's student for all 3 years of middle school, but she also earned our prestigious "Viking Trailblazer" award! Congratulations Katie!

Hopkins Middle School would like to recognize Chloe Dean for her outstanding achievement. As a seventh-grader, she has worked hard and achieved all A's. Great job Chloe! Keep working hard and finish middle school strong!

Hopkins Middle School would like to recognize Oliver Mead for his outstanding achievement. As a sixth-grader, he worked hard and achieved all A's. What a great way to start your middle school career! Keep up the great work, Oliver.

Katie, Chloe, and Oliver were not properly recognized in our All A's Ceremony video. We apologize for leaving your names off the list. Thank you for all your hard work!

--
Scott Stockwell
Middle School Principal
Hopkins Public Schools
269.793.7407 ex 3507

post_text: Tonight there was an unauthorized post made to the Hopkins Middle School FB page involving the number of worldwide deaths from the corona virus. Once discovered, it was removed and appears to have resulted from a hacked account. It is the belief of the school that as an educational institution, it is our duty to inform our students and families, not to persuade one way or the other on highly political issues. This unauthorized post was meant to persuade and therefore does not uphold the values of our institution. We apologize for the inconvenience.

post_text: With students at home now in an eLearning environment, Service Electric Cablevision (SECV) will offer 90 days free Internet service to students and teachers who do not already subscribe to SECV Internet. This offer includes the 40M x 4M tier of Internet service, along with a free cable modem rental. It applies only to new internet customers that have not subscribed to SECV Internet service within the last sixty (60) days and other restrictions may apply. Self-installation is required with the assistance of a technician outside the residence to ensure the safety of our employees. All new customers must provide written proof of their child’s current enrollment at a local school in SECV’s service area. All teachers must reside in SECV’s service area and provide evidence of current employment status. For more information or to enroll in the eLearning offer, please have students or teachers call us at 877.955.7328.

post_text:
Student Meal Information

On Monday, March 30th, Governor Wolf closed schools for “an indefinite period of time.” Beginning Wednesday, April 1st, in order to limit the time our staff is in the building and interacting with the public, the Lewisburg Area School District will be providing student meals on the days listed on this calendar link. The same number of meals will be distributed each week, just on what is essentially an every other day basis.

Distribution sites remain as they were previously:

· Kelly Elementary School – Parent drop off/pick up loop outside in the large parking lot
· Eichhorn Middle School – Bus loop in front of the school
· Downtown at the Former Lewisburg Area High School – School Bus stop under the side awning
· Click here for additional food delivery site information
Meals/food will be distributed to any school age child, regardless of paid, free, or reduced-price lunch status, provided they are present with their parent, guardian, or are able to show school identification if they come alone.

post_text:
This afternoon, Governor Wolf has announced that schools will remain closed for two additional weeks, with the Department of Education adding that the closures will last until at least April 6. Previously, he had shut them until March 30. The Department of Education said that, once schools are safe to open, administrators and teachers will have two days to prepare before students return on the third day.

The District’s administrative team will meet tomorrow morning to plan how to provide additional learning activities during this extended school closure. As these activities become available, we will communicate them to you.

Please follow all guidance from the Center for Disease Control, the Pennsylvania Department of Health, and the Governor to keep yourself and others safe.

LASD CLOSED

Staff will be in building offices beginning Tuesday to answer any questions you may have or for students to pick up any personal belongings.

Steve Skalka
Superintendent

Bucknell College of Engineering Sink or Swim 2019 Registration Form

Linntown Intermediate School
January 28, 2019 at 10:05 AM ·

Calling all bright-minded, young STEM enthusiasts! Bucknell University is inviting any and all girls that are in the 3rd to 6th grade range to our very first Sink or Swim: A Girls' Morning of Aquatic Innovation, Exploration, and FUN event! The event is happening from 8:30AM-12:00PM on Sunday, February 17th in Bucknell's KLARC Athletic Facilities. Participants will have the opportunity to maneuver underwater robots with Bucknell engineering students, play "pitch a product" games with Bucknell management students, develop team-leadership skills with some of Bucknell's Division I athletes, and so much more! We want young women to know that STEM is cool, and we wish to provide them with the confidence that, if they want to, they can pursue a future career in STEM! This event is free of charge to those who register! Please register online at https://tinyurl.com/BucknellSinkorSwim2019 by Wednesday, February 13th! Don't wait to register: there are a limited amount of spots available! We hope to see you there for a morning of education, innovation, and fun!
